questions.reason: Reason (R): It helps in detecting errors in ledger accounts and also in preparation of final accounts.
questions.assertion: Assertion (A): Trial Balance is a statement of all ledger accounts balances at a particular date.
questions.explanation: This option is correct because the trial balance is indeed a list of all ledger accounts and their balances, and it is used to detect errors and prepare final accounts.

questions.assertion: Assertion (A): Rectification of errors is the process of making adjustments in the ledger accounts to correct errors.

questions.reason: Reason (R): These errors affect the financial statement and may lead to serious consequences.
questions.assertion: Assertion (A): The following are the errors of principle: 1) Omitting a transaction, 2) Posting all transactions in the wrong side.
questions.explanation: This option is correct because omitting a transaction is an error of omission, not an error of principle. Posting all transactions in the wrong side is an error of commission, but it is not an error of principle. Errors of principle are serious errors that affect the financial statement and may lead to serious consequences, such as misrepresentation of financial position or results of operations.

questions.reason: Reason (R): This journal entry is the opposite of the original journal entry that caused the error.
questions.assertion: Assertion (A): Errors of commission can be rectified by reversing the journal entry.
questions.explanation: This option is correct because errors of commission can be rectified by reversing the journal entry, and this journal entry is indeed the opposite of the original journal entry that caused the error.

questions.reason: Reason (R): It occurs when a transaction is recorded at a lower amount than the actual amount.
questions.assertion: Assertion (A): Undercasting is a type of error of omission.
questions.explanation: This option is correct because undercasting is a type of error of commission, not an error of omission. Error of omission occurs when a transaction is omitted or not recorded at all, whereas error of commission occurs when a transaction is recorded at an incorrect amount.
